summary: Install-time host fingerprinting beacon to webhook.site in dependency-confusion PoC package
details: @sudoughnym/enviro-demo ships preinstall.js and postinstall.js lifecycle scripts that unconditionally POST installer host identifiers (os.hostname(), process.cwd(), process.pid, process.version, process.platform) to a hardcoded third-party collector at https://webhook.site/f83b073c-a04a-4ac5-8930-507051bd22f7 during `npm install`. The package's own package.json description self-identifies it as a HubSpot dependency-confusion proof-of-concept exploiting a semver version gap, and the postinstall console output references follow-on attacks (CI/CD secret theft, build backdoors). Regardless of the author's stated PoC framing, installing this package leaks installer identity to a third-party endpoint at install time, with no opt-out and no documented purpose visible to the installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
